tests.test_name: Coverage run produces a populated coverage table
tests.feature: Story vs. Test Coverage — real Claude scenarios
tests.journey: A user pastes a user story with acceptance criteria, uploads a BDD test fixture, runs coverage analysis, and verifies a populated coverage table is returned.

test_coverage_matrix.scenario: Coverage run produces a populated coverage table
test_coverage_matrix.given_context: User is on the Story Coverage page with a user story and BDD test fixture uploaded
test_coverage_matrix.then_assertion: Result contains a coverage table with at least one row and is non-empty

tests.test_name: Non-covered AC rows have a risk value
tests.feature: Story vs. Test Coverage — real Claude scenarios
tests.journey: A user runs a coverage analysis using an incomplete test fixture and verifies that every non-covered acceptance criteria row carries a risk value.

test_coverage_matrix.scenario: Non-covered AC rows have a risk value
test_coverage_matrix.given_context: User is on the Story Coverage page with the login story fixture and the incomplete test fixture uploaded
test_coverage_matrix.then_assertion: Every non-covered row in the coverage table has a non-empty risk value

tests.test_name: Fully covered AC rows have no risk value
tests.feature: Story vs. Test Coverage — real Claude scenarios
tests.journey: A user runs a coverage analysis using a complete test fixture and verifies that covered acceptance criteria rows do not carry any risk value.

test_coverage_matrix.scenario: Fully covered AC rows have no risk value
test_coverage_matrix.given_context: User is on the Story Coverage page with the login story fixture and the complete test fixture uploaded
test_coverage_matrix.then_assertion: No covered row in the coverage table has a risk value

tests.test_name: Markdown download works after coverage run
tests.feature: Story vs. Test Coverage — real Claude scenarios
tests.journey: A user who has already produced a coverage result downloads it as a Markdown file and verifies the correct file type is received.

test_coverage_matrix.scenario: Markdown download works after coverage run
test_coverage_matrix.given_context: User is on the Story Coverage page and a coverage result has been produced
test_coverage_matrix.when_action: User clicks the Download MD button

tests.test_name: HTML download works after coverage run
tests.feature: Story vs. Test Coverage — real Claude scenarios
tests.journey: A user who has already produced a coverage result downloads it as an HTML file and verifies the correct file type is received.

test_coverage_matrix.scenario: HTML download works after coverage run
test_coverage_matrix.given_context: User is on the Story Coverage page and a coverage result has been produced
test_coverage_matrix.when_action: User clicks the Download HTML button

tests.test_name: JSON download works after coverage run
tests.feature: Story vs. Test Coverage — real Claude scenarios
tests.journey: A user who has already produced a coverage result downloads it as a JSON file and verifies the correct file type is received.

test_coverage_matrix.scenario: JSON download works after coverage run
test_coverage_matrix.given_context: User is on the Story Coverage page and a coverage result has been produced
test_coverage_matrix.when_action: User clicks the Download JSON button
